segments.text: He entered and was passing through Jericho.
There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.
He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn’t because of the crowd, because he was short.
He ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was going to pass that way.
When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him,
Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.
When they saw it, they all murmured, saying,
He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord,
Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.
segments.speaker: Zacchaeus stood and

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that God’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately.
He said therefore,
A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He called ten servants of his and gave them ten mina coins, [^39] and told them, ‘Conduct business until I come.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to reign over us.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “When he had come back again, having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The first came before him, saying, ‘Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “He said to him, ‘Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “So he said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Another came, saying, ‘Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn’t lay down, and reap that which you didn’t sow.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “He said to him, ‘Out of your own mouth I will judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn’t lay down and reaping that which I didn’t sow.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to those who stood by, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to him who has the ten minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “They said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten minas!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But bring those enemies of mine who didn’t want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
When he came near to Bethsphage[^40] and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,
saying,
Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, which no man has ever sat upon. Untie it and bring it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ say to him: ‘The Lord needs it.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who were sent went away and found things just as he had told them.
As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them,
Why are you untying the colt?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said,
The Lord needs it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt and sat Jesus on them.
As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.
As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,
saying,
Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him,
Teacher, rebuke your disciples!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered them,
I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he came near, he saw the city and wept over it,
saying,
If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the days will come on you when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn’t know the time of your visitation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He entered into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,
saying to them,
It is written, ‘My house is a house of prayer,’but you have made it a ‘den of robbers’!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.
They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.
